name: Chicken Chettinad Kuzhambu

steps.instruction: Marinate chicken with turmeric powder, red chilli powder, salt, ginger garlic paste, and lemon juice or curd. Set aside.
Dry roast all Chettinad masala spices (red chillies, coriander seeds, fennel, cumin, pepper, cardamom, cinnamon, cloves, star anise, stone flower) until aromatic and golden. Cool down.
In the same pan, roast grated coconut and one sprig curry leaves with little oil until light golden.
Grind the roasted spices to a fine powder, then add roasted coconut, curry leaves, and cashews. Add ½ cup water and grind to a smooth paste.
Heat oil in a pan, add bay leaf and chopped onions. Sauté until onions turn light golden.
Add marinated chicken and fry on high heat for 4-5 minutes.
Add chopped tomatoes and sauté until oil separates and tomatoes are cooked.
Add the Chettinad masala paste, remaining curry leaves, and hot water. Mix well.
Cover and simmer until chicken is fully cooked and tender. Adjust consistency with more water if needed.
Taste and adjust salt. Garnish with fried curry leaves (optional) and serve hot.

tips: Use bone-in chicken for better flavour and authentic taste.
Roast spices on low heat and do not burn them to avoid bitterness.
Grind the masala paste very smooth for best texture.
This kuzhambu tastes even better the next day as flavours develop.
